text: Git branching is an essential part of version control that helps teams work on code without interfering with each other. When starting a new feature, developers create a feature branch from the main codebase. This allows them to make changes in isolation without affecting the stable version. Once the feature is complete, it can be merged back into the main branch. Before merging, teams often use pull requests to review the code and discuss any improvements. This workflow keeps the project organized, reduces errors, and makes collaboration smoother, especially when multiple people are working on different parts of the same project.
